title: Caller-supplied provenance can leave model-driven mutations on the default-allow path

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Untrusted provenance is a separate helper/boolean supplied by the caller rather than an invariant attached to the returned result or derived by the proxy.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Without an approval gate, a side-effecting call is blocked only when ctx.untrusted is true; false proceeds.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The admitted call reaches ToolExecutor::execute.

source: Model-selected ToolCall influenced by untrusted prompt, retrieved, or prior tool content; the integrating caller constructs InvokeContext.
closest_control: Optional ApprovalGate plus ctx.untrusted; ToolProxy neither authenticates the provenance assertion nor requires approval for side effects when the gate is absent.
sink: Execution of a registered side-effecting tool.
impact: A missing or incorrect taint assignment can turn untrusted model intent into a privileged mutation without human review.

candidate_local_validation.method: full-file source trace plus existing unit tests
candidate_local_validation.evidence: cargo test -p aog-toolproxy --offline passed 53 tests; tests at lib.rs:1098-1164 show protection only when the caller explicitly sets untrusted=true, while lib.rs:1230-1247 confirms untrusted=false with no inbox executes the mutation.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Untrusted content influencing model tool selection
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The caller omits/missets provenance or deploys ToolProxy without ApprovalGate
candidate_local_attack_path.path: model call -> caller-built InvokeContext -> optional provenance check -> executor

title: Mission call and spend ceilings are checked and charged non-atomically

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Mission::check reads calls/spend and Mission::record mutates them in a later operation.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: invoke releases the mission lock after check and can await approval before charging usage.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Usage is recorded only after approval, then the executor runs.

source: Concurrent calls sharing one ToolProxy mission.
closest_control: Mutexes serialize each check and each record but do not make check-and-reserve atomic across the approval await.
sink: Multiple admitted executions and cumulative spend beyond the mission ceiling.
impact: Parallel approved work can exceed configured mission call/spend limits, increasing blast radius and cost.

candidate_local_validation.method: static interleaving review plus sequential test inspection
candidate_local_validation.evidence: The lock is dropped at lib.rs:333, approval awaits at 350, and charging occurs at 400. Existing tests exercise only sequential ceiling behavior.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No defensive concurrency regression test exists in the assigned crate.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Workload able to issue concurrent side-effecting calls
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Mission ceiling configured and an asynchronous approval gate is present
candidate_local_attack_path.path: parallel checks observe the same tally -> approvals complete -> separate records -> executions

title: Operator blast-radius caps are checked and charged non-atomically

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Guardrails::check and TaskUsage::record are separate operations.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: invoke releases task_usage after the guard check and may await approval.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Task usage is charged later and admitted calls execute.

source: Concurrent calls using the same session id.
closest_control: The task_usage mutex protects individual map accesses, not an atomic reservation spanning approval and execution.
sink: Tool execution after multiple calls were admitted against the same pre-charge usage snapshot.
impact: Configured hard call/system blast-radius limits can be exceeded under concurrency.

candidate_local_validation.method: static interleaving review plus test inspection
candidate_local_validation.evidence: Check completes at lib.rs:303, task usage is unlocked, approval may await at 350, and record occurs only at 406. Existing tests cover sequential admission only.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No defensive concurrency regression test exists in the assigned crate.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Workload able to issue concurrent calls within one task
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Guard caps configured and calls overlap before usage recording
candidate_local_attack_path.path: parallel guard checks -> asynchronous review/work scheduling -> later records -> executions

title: Mission system allowlist is bypassed when target-system metadata is absent

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The system allowlist is consulted only for Some(system); None is explicitly unconstrained.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: InvokeContext.system is caller-supplied optional metadata.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Executor receives the call arguments but not an independently derived target-system binding.

source: A call whose actual destination is represented in tool semantics/arguments while InvokeContext.system is None.
closest_control: Mission allowed_systems; omission is treated as no constraint rather than fail-closed or tool-derived binding.
sink: Execution against a system outside the declared mission envelope.
impact: Mission-scoped system restrictions may not constrain real filesystem, network, provider, or MCP targets.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: mission.rs:134-141 has no else/denial for None, and ToolExecutor::execute at lib.rs:104-109 has no context parameter that would force the declared system to match the actual target.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No deployed ToolExecutor/caller in the reviewed reachability set shows whether production always supplies and independently validates system metadata.

candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: A deployed executor can choose a protected system while the integration leaves ctx.system unset
candidate_local_attack_path.path: call with absent system metadata -> mission check skips axis -> executor

title: Distinct-system blast-radius cap ignores calls with absent system metadata

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Both the limit check and usage record operate only when ctx.system is Some.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: System identity is optional caller-supplied context.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Admitted tool execution can touch a destination not counted by the cap.

source: Calls that omit optional system metadata while their tool behavior touches a system.
closest_control: max_systems_per_task, but None neither trips the cap nor records a system.
sink: Execution and repeated fan-out without distinct-system accounting.
impact: The operator's system blast-radius cap may provide no bound for unlabelled calls.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: guard.rs:120-126 and 143-145 both require Some(system); no fallback derives a system from the tool definition or arguments.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No deployed ToolExecutor/caller in the reviewed reachability set establishes whether system metadata is mandatory before invoke.

candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Integration permits omitted system metadata for system-touching tools
candidate_local_attack_path.path: unlabelled call -> cap check/record skip -> executor
candidate_local_attack_path.outcome: Unbounded system fan-out relative to configured cap

title: Credential revocation is not guaranteed when an invocation is cancelled

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Credential TTL is accepted from the minter and revoke is an asynchronous best-effort method.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The credential is minted before executor await and revoked only on the normal continuation after that await.

source: Cancellation, task abortion, shutdown, or panic after mint and before the explicit revoke call.
closest_control: Executor timeout and broker TTL; timeout does not protect cancellation of invoke, and ToolProxy does not enforce an upper TTL or cancellation-safe cleanup.
sink: A still-valid external credential lease after the local invocation has ended.
impact: Call-scoped authority can outlive the call and remain usable until external expiry/revocation.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: There is no scope guard or cancellation handler between mint at 412 and revoke at 429; dropping the future skips subsequent code.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No production CredentialMinter implementation or enforced maximum lease TTL was found in the reviewed caller search, so residual lifetime and external usability are not calibrated.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Caller or failure condition able to cancel an in-flight invocation
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: A production minter issues a credential with nontrivial TTL
candidate_local_attack_path.path: mint -> invocation cancelled -> normal revoke continuation not polled

title: Tool error text bypasses egress scanning and is copied into receipts

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Only result.output is passed to EgressScanner.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The unscanned ToolResult is returned and result.error is cloned into the receipt.

source: Untrusted executor/backend error strings that may contain secrets or regulated content.
closest_control: EgressScanner is always on for output, but the sibling error field has no scan/redaction path.
sink: Model-facing ToolResult.error and the receipt chain's error field.
impact: Sensitive error content can enter model context and durable governance evidence despite the redaction invariant.

candidate_local_validation.method: static field-flow review plus test coverage inspection
candidate_local_validation.evidence: All 53 crate tests passed, but scanner tests and invoke scanning cover output only; append_receipt copies result.error unchanged at line 525.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Untrusted tool/backend controlling an error response
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Executor exposes backend error text in ToolResult.error
candidate_local_attack_path.path: executor error -> output-only scan -> returned result and receipt

title: Sensitive JSON object keys bypass tool-result redaction

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Object values are recursively scanned while keys are cloned verbatim.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The partially scanned JSON value is returned to the model-facing caller.

source: Untrusted tool output whose object property names contain secret or regulated strings.
closest_control: EgressScanner redacts string values but intentionally excludes keys to preserve shape; no alternate key encoding/redaction or rejection exists.
sink: Returned ToolResult.output consumed by the model/agent loop.
impact: Credentials or regulated text placed in field names remain visible despite the stated every-string redaction guarantee.

candidate_local_validation.method: static exact-branch review plus test inspection
candidate_local_validation.evidence: scan.rs:125-129 clones each key unchanged; the nested JSON test at 407-424 asserts benign key preservation but has no sensitive-key negative case.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Untrusted tool/backend controlling JSON field names
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Tool output supports attacker-influenced object keys
candidate_local_attack_path.path: tool JSON -> value-only scanner -> unchanged key returned

title: Post-execution result scanning has no explicit size or depth ceiling

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Scanner recursively clones arrays/objects and allocates finding/redaction vectors without an input budget.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: tool.timeout bounds only executor.execute; scanning starts after the timeout returns.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Unbounded post-processing occurs on the caller task before receipt/return.

source: Oversized or deeply nested ToolResult.output from an untrusted executor/backend.
closest_control: Executor timeout; there is no independent byte/node/depth/work ceiling around scan_result.
sink: CPU, heap, or stack consumption in recursive scan and cloning.
impact: A tool/backend can stall or terminate the agent loop after executor timeout enforcement has completed.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: No maximum size/depth/node count is accepted or checked anywhere in scan_result/redact_value; timeout scope ends before scanning.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No deployed executor framing limit or service-level request cancellation boundary was reviewed, and no defensive large-result test was run.

candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Able to return a large in-memory JSON value through ToolExecutor
candidate_local_attack_path.path: executor returns within timeout -> unbounded recursive scan/clone
